---
title: 2.2a Line Chart Change in Wage Sector Facet
description: Relative change in sectoral employment levels since the Great Recession
date: '2026-05-07'
layout: default
permalink: /charts/rural-economic-outlook-line-chart-change-in-wage-sector-facet/
categories: []
tags: []
image: 2.2A_line_chart_change_in_wage_sector_facet.svg
chart:
  title: 2.2a Line Chart Change in Wage Sector Facet
  chartType: line
  interactive: false
  dataSource: Bureau of Labor Statistics, Quarterly Census of Employment and Wages
  sourceUrl: https://www.bls.gov/cew/
  featured: false
producedBy: rural-economic-outlook
usesDatasets:
  - qcew-employment-wages
usesPackages:
  - cori-data-bds
  - cori-data-fcc
  - ruraldefinitions
usesResources: []
format:
  gfm:
    toc: false
    wrap: none
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

![Relative change in sectoral employment levels since the Great Recession](./2.2A_line_chart_change_in_wage_sector_facet.svg)

## Overview

Relative change in sectoral employment levels since the Great Recession

## Key Findings

- Rural wage growth outpaces nonrural in relative terms since 2007
- Wage levels remain persistently lower in rural areas

## Reproducibility

Generated by `sector wage job changes_A.R` in the producing project.
